title: ranunculus baudotii subsp. confusus
intro: Ranunculus baudotii subsp. confusus, also known as the mountain crowfoot, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus baudotii subsp. confusus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
